openRisks: Scout does not currently persist canonical exact data items, relationship sets, attribution paths, outcome events, or vectors as first-class local storage.
Selector connectors are in-process fetchers and do not currently write canonical source records through the local REST API.
The older scout-n8n-node package appears to target a stale ingestion route.
Enterprise/Fortress live LanceDB and pgvector provider paths require explicit native-store proof and are not routine local checks.
pgvector write integration is documented as a placeholder in the audited private vector write seam.
A deterministic mapping from Scout tenant/workspace/user/source/fact IDs to Fortress postgres_pk/entity_type/layer/citation/provenance IDs is not defined yet.
Replay or backfill through current Scout ingestion could trigger billing limits, duplicate suppression, recompute jobs, and normal audit telemetry.
Cloud can gate entitlement and package access but must not receive raw data, vectors, evidence packs, relationship sets, attribution paths, or derived relationship intelligence.
nextPrompt: Create docs/work-packages/wp2-upgrade-and-lancedb-migration/02-migration-contract-and-adapter-plan.md. Use 01-discovery-audit.md as the source of truth. Define the Scout-to-Enterprise/Fortress migration contract, canonical local data item model, relationship set and attribution path model, outcome event model, vector ID/layer mapping, connector write-routing decision, backfill/replay rules, Cloud entitlement gates, rollback boundaries, and proof criteria for LanceDB/pgvector. Do not make code changes until the contract is reviewed.
